title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Colonial home at 1210 Washington St, Abington, built in 1950, sold for $500,000 on a 14,792 sq ft lot.
In Arlington, a ranch home at 203 Everett St, built in 1956, sold for $1,975,000 with 1,409 sq ft and 3 bedrooms.
A high-rise condo at 100 Belvidere St, Boston, built in 2001, sold for $3,700,000 featuring 1,692 sq ft and 3 baths.

title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alec Pierce may enter free agency after Colts used transition tag on QB Daniel Jones instead.
Pierce is a top free-agent receiver, with 47 catches for 1,003 yards, and led NFL in yards per catch.
Patriots have $39.2 million in cap space; signing Pierce could necessitate restructuring Stefon Diggs' contract.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Natasha Morisseau, 34, a Vermont postal worker, was cited for petty larceny and unlawful mischief for discarding mail.
Police found opened and unopened mail in a North Troy dumpster, including holiday cards with money, linked to Morisseau.
Morisseau has been suspended since Jan. 23, with the case under review by USPS Inspector General; court set for March 17.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An elderly man and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street in Foxborough early Tuesday.
The fire was reported just before 3 a.m., and the home was heavily damaged by the blaze.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville firefighters managed to contain most of the fire within an hour.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston restaurant Estella's liquor license is threatened after a party with Patriots players featuring strippers and hookahs.
Owner Helder George Brandao told the Boston Licensing Board the incident spiraled out of control, blaming player entitlement.
Police found evidence of after-hours drinking, smoking, and nudity; the board is considering license suspension or revocation.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest nationwide scores on AP exams, setting a new all-time record.
35.8% of Massachusetts public high school graduates scored 3 or higher on an AP exam, highest ever recorded.
Massachusetts ranked first nationwide in AP scores and third in AP exam participation, with significant subsidies for low-income students.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Councilors received a 50% pay raise approved by the previous council, increasing salaries from $29,128.65 to $44,500.
Newly elected councilors, who campaigned against the raises, plan to donate their increase to charities and nonprofit organizations.
Quincy councilors cannot immediately repeal the raises due to state laws and city charter restrictions, despite efforts to address the issue.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox remain a potential trade partner for Astros' third baseman Isaac Paredes despite acquiring Caleb Durbin.
A trade involving Jarren Duran could create designated hitter opportunities for Masataka Yoshida and Triston Casas.
No recent contact between Red Sox and Astros for Paredes, despite his appealing power potential at Fenway Park.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's mock draft predicts Patriots will select Texas A&M edge rusher Cashius Howell at No. 31 overall.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season and led the SEC in total pressures, showing strong pass-rushing abilities.
New England seeks to bolster its pass rush, aiming to replace potential free agent loss K’Lavon Chaisson.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, convicted of first-degree murder for killing Salem State student Claire Gravel in 1986.
Carey was indicted in 2022, with DNA evidence linking him to the crime following a new lead in 2012.
Carey, already serving time for a 2008 attempted murder, faces life without parole for Gravel's murder.
